question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from patient samples?
explain: The paper discusses the analysis of HIV-1 sequences from 726 antiretroviral-naïve patients infected with non-B HIV-1 subtypes. It mentions the collection of samples from various clinical centers and the subsequent sequencing of these samples.
sentences: "The prevalence of ETR RAMs in 726 antiretroviral-naïve patients infected with non-B HIV-1 subtypes was studied."; "In total, 726 patients from the following centers were included (numbers of patients from each center are indicated in parentheses): CESAC (Centre d\'Ecoute, de Soins, d\'Animation et de Conseils) in Bamako, Mali (163); Nianankoro Fomba Hospital in Ségou, Mali (118); Pitié-Salpêtrière Hospital in Paris, France (192); Bichat Claude-Bernard Hospital in Paris, France (182); and Saint-Antoine Hospital in Paris, France (71)."; "For each patient, a single HIV-1 sequence was included."

question: Does the paper report in vitro drug susceptibility data?
explain: The paper includes a section on phenotypic study where it discusses the phenotypic susceptibilities of samples with at least one ETR RAM to nevirapine (NVP), efavirenz (EFV), and ETR. It also provides specific data on the changes in 50% inhibitory concentrations (IC50) for these drugs.
sentences: "NNRTI phenotypic susceptibilities of samples with at least one ETR RAM were measured."; "Samples with at least one ETR RAM were tested for phenotypic susceptibility to nevirapine (NVP), efavirenz (EFV), and ETR."; "Phenotypic results were available for 20 clinical samples harboring at least one ETR RAM (Table ​ (Table1). 1 )."
